danger_signals.meaning: Lemmings die from falling too great a height; visual level design communicates safe vs lethal drop distances

danger_signals.visual_form: Booby traps, fire, lava pools rendered in level tileset
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial obstacles that kill lemmings on contact; environmental visual placement signals danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Lava pools and water features in terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Lemmings drown or burn in these environments; color/texture distinction from safe ground
When timer expires, all lemmings explode regardless of status; visual countdown communicates urgency
Lemmings fall off bottom edge and die; spatial limit communicated through level framing

danger_signals.visual_form: Lemming sprite with bomber skill active, 5-second countdown animation
danger_signals.meaning: Assigned lemming will self-destruct after visible timer; player-induced danger signal

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated exit point marked in level geometry
safety_signals.meaning: Goal destination for lemmings; reaching this space signals level progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Level ground with no hazards or drops
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage area where lemmings can walk without danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Lemming sprite with parachute deployed during descent
safety_signals.meaning: Safe fall mechanic; visual indicator that height danger is negated

safety_signals.visual_form: Stationary lemming sprite positioned in path
safety_signals.meaning: Creates safe stopping point; other lemmings reverse direction on contact instead of falling

safety_signals.visual_form: Builder lemming constructing 12-step stairway, visible brick accumulation
safety_signals.meaning: Creates safe elevated passage; visual construction progress shows traversable path forming

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple green-haired, blue-robed lemmings moving together in formation
safety_signals.meaning: Following behavior; safe to allow passive movement toward exit along established paths

faction_indicators.color_identity: Green hair, blue robes (standard lemming appearance)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spawn from trap doors at level start; exit point is faction destination

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Traps, lava, fire rendered as level obstacles; no faction identity, pure environmental threat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout level geometry; not faction-aligned but act as opposing force to lemming survival

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trap doors open from above; steady stream of lemmings begins descending
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemmings follow each other in procession; default walking behavior without skill assignment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lemmings interact with assigned skills; terrain changes from skill use (mining, building, bashing)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Modified lemming movement patterns based on active skills; some lemmings climb, dig, or block

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trap animations, fire, lava visual effects trigger on contact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemmings die or are removed from screen; damage/death animations play

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer display counts down visibly; urgent audio cues (implied)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining lemmings continue movement; all will explode when timer reaches zero

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lemmings reaching exit point; score/evaluation screen display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemmings enter exit and disappear; level transition occurs if save threshold met

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Level failure (time expired or insufficient saves)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All lemmings explode simultaneously; screen effects and visual chaos
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All remaining lemmings removed; level reset or retry prompt shown

environmental_storytelling.example: Level geometry itself tells the puzzle story—booby traps, lava pools, and drop distances are arranged to force player creative problem-solving with limited skills
Levels grouped into four difficulty categories; visual complexity and hazard density increase, signaling difficulty escalation without text
Lemmings' default walking, turning on obstacles, and falling behavior establishes world physics; player learns environmental rules through lemming interaction
Mining, bashing, and building skills show terrain destruction and construction in real-time; landscape visually changes in response to player actions
Unused trap animations and removed traps suggest iteration in level design; remaining traps signal intentional hazard placement for puzzle logic

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks in one direction, ignores other lemmings (except Blockers), falls off edges, turns around when hitting obstacles
Climbs vertically, but falls if encountering overhang; visual upward movement signals vertical pathway availability
Deploys parachute and falls safely from heights; visual parachute sprite indicates height danger negation
Explodes after timer expires; destroys destructible landscape nearby but not other lemmings; countdown animation signals imminent self-destruction
Stands still permanently; incoming lemmings reverse direction on contact; provides safe stopping mechanism
Constructs 12-step stairway upward; stops when hitting overhang or obstacle; visual brick accumulation shows progress
Horizontally (basher), diagonally (miner), or vertically (digger) tunnels through terrain; cannot break steel barriers; visual path creation signals terrain destruction capability
Dies on impact; removal from screen signals failure state
Dies or is captured; visual hazard animation plays; removal from screen
All explode simultaneously regardless of state; visual explosion effects across entire screen

notes: Lemmings (NES) is a port of the Amiga original with noted technical limitations ('sluggish reactions, inaccurate gameplay mechanics'). The game communicates world-state entirely through environmental and behavioral visuals without explicit faction systems or political narrative. Danger/safety signals are purely mechanical and spatial. The core manifestation layer relies on: (1) spatial geometry as hazard/safety indicator, (2) lemming sprite and animation states as behavioral communication, (3) skill assignment visual feedback showing world alteration, (4) HUD timer as urgency signal. No color-coded faction systems exist; lemmings are unified player-controlled entities vs. environmental hazards. Unused trap animations and removed traps (documented in TCRF source) suggest original design had more hazard variety, but NES version simplified manifestation language. Manifestation paradigm is entirely mechanical/puzzle-focused rather than narrative or political.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Lemmings NES article)
